# references on notes: note ref add/rm/list + cross-store backlink + creation-time --ref
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init
stdout 'initialized'

exec mtt add 'A'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt add 'B'
stdout 'created t2'
exec mtt note add auth-design
stdout 'created auth-design'

# a note references a task (target exists -> ok)
exec mtt note ref add auth-design task:t2
stdout 'added task:t2 to auth-design'

# the note's outgoing ref is listed
exec mtt note ref list auth-design
stdout 'refs:'
stdout 'task:t2  \[ok\]'

# cross-store: task t2 now has an incoming backlink FROM the note
exec mtt ref list t2
stdout 'backlinks:'
stdout 'note:auth-design'

# note ref rm is idempotent (twice, both exit 0)
exec mtt note ref rm auth-design task:t2
stdout 'removed task:t2 from auth-design'
exec mtt note ref rm auth-design task:t2
stdout 'removed task:t2 from auth-design'

# note ref add on a missing note -> not found (exit 4)
! exec mtt note ref add ghost task:t2
stderr 'not found'

# creation-time --ref on note add (canonicalized, verified/warned)
exec mtt note add spec --ref task:t1 --ref task:t1
stdout 'created spec'
exec mtt note ref list spec
stdout 'task:t1  \[ok\]'

# creation-time --ref on task add; a dangling one warns but stores (exit 0)
exec mtt add 'C' --ref task:t1 --ref task:t999
stdout 'created t3'
stderr 'warning: task:t999 is dangling'
exec mtt ref list t3
stdout 'task:t1  \[ok\]'
stdout 'task:t999  \[dangling\]'
